notes: Oversized-transcript-line robustness for mistral-vibe: one prompt eliciting a single large single-line assistant reply (~30 KB, ≥5000 words, no embedded newlines), asserting the ready→working→ready arc with no parser stall. The oversized-line protection is SHARED tailer code, not a vibe behavior: TailAndProcess calls readLineCapped(reader, maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB) BEFORE json.Unmarshal decodes the line for vibe's JSONLineParser (issue #270 hardening: bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped, NOT bufio.Scanner) — so the JSONL path is gated by exactly the same 64 MB cap as every adapter; lines under 64 MB are read whole and resolved via the usual turn_done close, lines over it are logged-and-skipped past without stalling. TruncateAssistantText caps only the display AssistantText; ParseLine still receives the full decoded map, so the truncation never affects the arc. ROBUSTNESS CLAIM: the 'oversized line' size property is NOT a phase-DSL form — the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from this clean arc completing (no stuck working, no waiting). The genuine >64 MB cap behavior is pinned agent-agnostically by core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go; a live vibe CLI cannot be coerced into a multi-MB single line, so this recording is a sanity-check on a large single-line turn (a synthetic follow-up cell can hand-craft a >2 MB line if deeper cap-stress is needed). Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<...> transcript directory, sessionIDFromPath), modeled like claudecode/kiro-cli — birth anchored to start, unpinned; whether a proc-<PID> presession row precedes it is emission-dependent and record settles it. Model mistral-medium-3.5 confirmed from live config.active_model; tokens observable (sidecar stats.context_tokens → turn_done TokenSnapshot); cost NOT (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity), so cost_nonzero=false. Recipe needs wait_turn, which the scaffolded driver stubs (driver=gap:wait_turn); record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving. BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter -- birth re-modeled as presession_birth -> session_birth (new_session, different session_id) templated from the codex sibling; observations now assert agent only (cost & tokens are sidecar-only, unverifiable in the offline replay golden, like kiro-cli), superseding any model/tokens claim above.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
Prompt eliciting one large single-line reply drives ready -> working

Oversized assistant line is read whole under the 64 MB cap (or skipped past, over it) and parsed without stalling -> turn completes via turn_done, working -> ready; state never entered waiting. Completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof: if the tailer had wedged on the long line this would never fire.
